security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be altered without detection.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks input validation and tamper detection mechanisms for votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate or check the integrity of vote messages.
The actor can modify the vote message before submission.

mitigation.strategy: Implement input validation and tamper detection measures for vote messages.

description: An attacker could alter a vote to change its outcome.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be cast without verifiable identification of the voter.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not have a mechanism to prove who submitted a particular vote.

assumptions: The governance contract does not record voter identity.
The actor is able to cast a vote anonymously.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure authentication and logging for vote submission.

description: An attacker could cast votes and deny responsibility for those actions.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be disclosed to unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not store votes securely.
The database or communication channel is vulnerable to unauthorized access.

mitigation.strategy: Secure the storage and transmission of vote data using encryption and access controls.

description: An attacker could intercept or retrieve vote information.
